project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Validate enlarged mobile AR solar scale, selected-planet GLB detail preview, and clean close-to-landing remount across devices.

lastAchievements: Raised mobile AR solar scale again: SOLAR_FALLBACK_SCALE is now 0.42 and touch multipliers are 1.95-2.45 with a 2.7 clamp.
Replaced the CSS-drawn detail preview with an embedded static A-Frame preview that loads the selected planet model from planet.modelPath under public/assets/models/planets.
Added GLB preview loading/ready/error states so panel fallback is text-only instead of generated planet art.
Stored active GSAP planet timelines and killed them during scanner boot/teardown so late onComplete callbacks cannot mutate the landing page after close.
Expanded AR document side-effect cleanup for A-Frame body classes and inline layout/touch/visibility styles.
Kept scanner close recovery as a clean landing shell remount plus delayed artifact cleanup timers.
Updated DESIGN, design-intent, flow overview, and ADR to match larger mobile scale, GLB detail preview, and close cleanup behavior.
pendingIssues: Real-device verification is still required on Android Chrome, iOS Safari, and tablets after the latest larger scale changes.
If the solar row is still too small or too large, adjust getSolarScaleMultiplier or SOLAR_FALLBACK_SCALE from the current 0.42 baseline.
If planet taps feel offset after scale changes, tune hitZonePosition and hitZoneRadius in src/data/planets.ts.
Verify embedded GLB preview sizing across planets; tweak previewScale or PANEL_PREVIEW_TARGET_SIZE values if any appear too large or too small.
If close still fails on a specific browser, capture DevTools source dimensions, body/html class list, app DOM after close, and whether a killed timeline callback still fires.
nextBestActions: Deploy and test open AR -> scan Hiro -> tap planet -> confirm selected local GLB preview -> close detail -> close scanner -> reopen AR on iOS and Android.
Verify the landing page and modal remain centered and tappable after closing AR and after reopening AR.
Verify the enlarged solar row feels readable on iPhone-sized and Infinix Note 40-sized viewports without unacceptable clipping.
